response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but their profile does not match this role's core requirements. Their C++ experience is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no SQL Server/Oracle depth, no Fixed Income or derivatives exposure, and only tangential financial-services experience (RIDE Capital, a Node.js/TypeScript stack). The gap on both the mandatory technical stack and the required domain expertise makes this a poor fit despite otherwise solid engineering credentials.
strengths: Broad senior-level engineering experience across web, backend, and systems programming
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, DevOps, and knowledge sharing
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~6 months, a decade ago) versus the mandatory 5–10 years
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or substantive capital-markets domain experience
No demonstrated SQL Server/Oracle or Windows-platform development background
